context: Tests whether the agent produces a PRD following the create-prd skill conventions: correct file location, required 10 sections, content focused on what/why rather than how, and no premature code.

checklist.name: File saved to /tasks/
checklist.description: The PRD file is saved at /tasks/prd-[feature-name].md (in the /tasks/ directory with a prd- prefix)

checklist.name: User Stories section
checklist.description: The PRD contains a 'User Stories' section

checklist.name: Functional Requirements section
checklist.description: The PRD contains a 'Functional Requirements' section

checklist.name: Design and Technical Considerations
checklist.description: The PRD contains both a 'Design Considerations' section and a 'Technical Considerations' section

checklist.name: Implementation Surface section
checklist.description: The PRD contains an 'Implementation Surface' section describing entry points, architectural layer, or primary components

checklist.name: Success Metrics section
checklist.description: The PRD contains a 'Success Metrics' section

checklist.name: Open Questions section
checklist.description: The PRD contains an 'Open Questions' section

checklist.name: No implementation code
checklist.description: The PRD does NOT contain any code blocks, code snippets, or implementation-level code

checklist.name: What/why focus
checklist.description: The PRD describes WHAT should be built and WHY, not HOW — requirements are expressed as outcomes or user needs rather than technical steps or specific implementations

checklist.name: Next steps suggestion
checklist.description: The agent suggests at least one next step from: generate-tasks, ticket-planning, rails-architecture-review, rails-engine-author, or rails-stack-conventions
